name: Isosorbide mononitrate
executiveSummary: Long-acting nitrate for chronic angina and heart failure.
indications:
## Indications

- Chronic stable angina pectoris.
- Heart failure (adjunctive in institutional regimens).
- Prophylaxis of effort-induced angina.

## Precautions

- Hypotension, frequent headache at the start of treatment.
- Contraindicated with PDE-5 inhibitors (sildenafil, etc.).
- Nitrate-free interval required if used multiple times daily (to prevent tolerance).

> Adjust per institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 30–120 mg/day PO in 1–2 doses (extended-release formulation preferred).
adulto.administration: PO. Do not crush extended-release tablets.

pediatrico.dose: Limited pediatric use; follow cardiological protocol.

stability:
## Stability

- Store according to package insert; protect from moisture.
adverseEffects:
## Effects

- Headache, hypotension, dizziness, facial flushing, reflex tachycardia.

bibliography.citation: American Heart Association. Angina and heart failure guidelines.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Argentine Society of Cardiology. Clinical practice guidelines.
American Academy of Pediatrics. Medication guidance in critical care.
